Let $A$ be an $n \times n$ matrix and let $\alpha$ be an $n$-dimensional vector
such that $A\alpha = \alpha$. Suppose that all the entries of $A$ and $\alpha$
are positive real numbers. Prove that $\alpha$ is the only linearly independent
eigenvector of $A$ corresponding to the eigenvalue 1. Hint: if $\beta$ is
another eigenvector, consider the minimum of $\alpha_i /|\beta_i|$, $i = 1,
\dots, n$, where the $\alpha_i$'s and $\beta_i$'s are the components of
$\alpha$ and $\beta$, respectively.
